programme.title: Saudi Arabian F1 GP Hlts 2021
programme.desc: The 2021 Saudi Arabian Grand Prix, held at the Jeddah Corniche Circuit. A full night race, this is the first time this event has ever taken place.

programme.title: Saudi Arabian F1 GP Hlts 2022
programme.desc: The Formula 1 season continues with the 2022 Saudi Arabian Grand Prix. After a Red Bull implosion and a Ferrari one-two in Bahrain, the Jeddah Corniche Circuit is set to provide fireworks.

programme.title: Saudi Arabian F1 GP Hlts 2023
programme.desc: On the front row, pole-sitter Sergio Perez is joined by Fernando Alonso as George Russell completes the top three for the Saudi Arabian Grand Prix. Max Verstappen starts from P15. (19.

programme.title: Saudi Arabian F1 GP Hlts 2024
programme.desc: The lights go out at the 2024 Saudi Arabian Grand Prix, staged at the Jeddah Corniche Circuit. For the fifth race in a row, Max Verstappen and Charles Leclerc share the front row. (09.

programme.title: Australian F1 GP Highlights
programme.desc: It's lights out for the season-opening Australian Grand Prix. George Russell leads a Mercedes front-row lockout with Red Bull's Isack Hadjar third. Lando Norris starts from P6. (08.

programme.title: Chinese F1 GP: Sprint
programme.desc: Shanghai sees the first of six F1 Sprints this season as George Russell leads a Mercedes front row with Lando Norris third. The fast-starting Ferraris line up in P4 and P6. (14.

programme.title: Chinese F1 GP Highlights

programme.title: Live UEFA Champions League
programme.sub-title: Bayern Munich v Real Madrid-Quarter Final, Second Leg
programme.desc: Bayern Munich v Real Madrid (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the quarter-final second-leg tie from Allianz Arena, as the two European giants go head to head for a place in the semi-finals. Madrid have won this competition a record 15 times and have triumphed in the final on eight occasions this century. Bayern have had plenty of success in this tournament as well having lifted the trophy six times, last winning the title in the Covid-hit 2019/20 season.

programme.desc: Aston Villa v Bologna (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the quarter-final second-leg tie from Villa Park.
